channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Sabalenka beats Keys at China Open and equals her career-best win streak
item.description: Second-ranked Aryna Sabalenka has matched her career-best 15 straight wins after beating Madison Keys 6-4, 6-3 to advance to the quarterfinals of the China Open.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gonzaga set to join rebuilt Pac-12, while UTEP goes to Mountain West
item.description: Rebuilding the Pac-12 Conference centered around football. It'll be a pretty good basketball conference, too, after landing one of the perennial hoops powers on the West Coast.
item.dc:creator: TIM BOOTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tatis hits 2-run homer, King strikes out 12 as the Padres beat the Braves 4-0 in playoff opener
item.description: Fernando Tatis Jr. hit a towering two-run homer on his first playoff swing in four years and Michael King struck out 12 in his first postseason start as the San Diego Padres beat rookie AJ Smith-Shawver and the Atlanta Braves 4-0 in Game 1 of their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: Bernie Wilson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Liberty top Aces 88-84 to take a 2-0 lead in the WNBA semifinals and put the champs on the brink
item.description: Sabrina Ionescu scored 24 points and the New York Liberty beat the Las Vegas Aces 88-84 to take a 2-0 lead in the best-of-five semifinal series Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Japanese sponsors Toyota, Bridgestone and Panasonic end Olympic contracts
item.description: The International Olympic Committee’s three major Japanese sponsors — Toyota, Panasonic and Bridgestone — are terminating their contracts.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pete Rose found home in Las Vegas before nation began to embrace sports betting
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Betting on baseball got Pete Rose banned from the game, never to return while he was alive. But, perhaps appropriately, he found a home in the nation's gambling capital — where acceptance wasn't a problem.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adams says no word from Pierce since Raiders coach appeared to like trade talk on social media
item.description: HENDERSON, Nev. (AP) — Davante Adams said on his weekly spot on the “Up and Adams Show” that he hadn't heard from Las Vegas coach Antonio Pierce since a screenshot appeared of Pierce appearing to like a social media post about possibly trading the Raiders star wide receiver.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Skubal shines in playoff debut to help Detroit Tigers to first playoff win in more than a decade
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Tarik Skubal added perhaps his most important accomplishment to an already sparkling resume.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Justin Verlander left off Astros' Wild Card Series roster, but Yordan Alvarez is back
item.description: The Houston Astros left three-time Cy Young Award winner Justin Verlander off the roster for their AL Wild Card Series against Detroit but did include injured slugger Yordan Alvarez. Verlander struggled in his return after missing almost two months with a neck injury this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani and the MLB playoffs, a pairing the world will experience for the 1st time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani was already a superstar with his hitting and pitching accomplishments over six years with the Los Angeles Angels. Yet every October, Ohtani vanished, with the Angels having a losing record and never making the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reports: Pete Rose, baseball's all-time hits leader, dead at 83
item.description: LAS VEGAS — Pete Rose, one of Major League Baseball's most polarizing athletes and the game's all-time hits leader, has died at the age of 83, according to TMZ. Rose's agent, Ryan Fiterman, confirmed the news to TMZ and said, "the family is asking for privacy at this time.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Xfinity playoffs begin as Aric Almirola dominates Kansas Speedway
item.description: Aric Almirola clinched his second win of the 2024 NASCAR Xfinity Series season at the Kansas Lottery 300, held at Kansas Speedway.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, NewsNation

item.title: Dikembe Mutombo, a Hall of Fame player, dies at 58 from brain cancer
item.description: Mutombo, a Basketball Hall of Famer who was one of the best defensive players in NBA history, died Monday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Broncos running back Tyler Badie carted from sideline with a back injury
item.description: Denver Broncos running back Tyler Badie was placed on a back board and carted from the sideline before the start of the second quarter against the New York Jets on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox beat playoff-bound Tigers 4-0, stay at 121 losses going into finale
item.description: Sean Burke pitched two-hit ball over five innings, Andrew Benintendi tied his career high with his 20th homer and the Chicago White Sox beat the playoff-bound Detroit Tigers 4-0 on Saturday to stay at 121 losses going into their season finale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox look to break Tigers' home win streak
item.description: Chicago White Sox (39-121, fifth in the AL Central) vs. Detroit Tigers (86-74, second in the AL Central) Detroit; Saturday, 1:10 p.m. EDT PITCHING PROBABLES: White Sox: Sean Burke (0-0); Tigers: TBD BOTTOM LINE: The Detroit Tigers, on a five-game home winning streak, host the Chicago White Sox.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles Chargers to sell minority stake in team to Detroit Pistons owner
item.description: More than a quarter stake in the Los Angeles Chargers, which has essentially been a family business for decades, will be sold to an NBA owner in the coming weeks, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Chicago Sky fire head coach Teresa Weatherspoon
item.description: The Chicago Sky have fired head coach Teresa Weatherspoon after a disappointing season in which the team went 13-27 and missed the WNBA playoff picture.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
